core_concept.explanation: sp hybridization occurs when one $s$ orbital mixes with one $p$ orbital to form two equivalent $sp$ hybrid orbitals. These orbitals arrange themselves $180^\circ$ apart (linear geometry) to minimize repulsion. The two remaining unhybridized $p$ orbitals are perpendicular to the hybrid orbitals and each other, enabling the formation of two $\pi$ bonds (triple bonds).

image.alt: Diagram showing one s and one p orbital mixing to form two sp orbitals
image.caption: Mixing one s and one p orbital yields two sp hybrids and two unhybridized p orbitals

sections.introduction: sp hybridization is observed in atoms with a steric number of 2, typically involving triple bonds (alkynes) or two double bonds (cumulated dienes). This hybridization state results in the most s-character (50%) of any common hybrid, leading to shorter, stronger bonds.

introduction_image.alt: Linear geometry showing 180 degree bond angle
introduction_image.caption: sp hybrid orbitals orient linearly at 180 degrees

key_concept.definition: sp hybrid orbitals are formed by the linear combination of one s and one p atomic orbital, resulting in two collinear hybrids.
key_concept.context: Commonly found in alkynes (C≡C), nitriles (C≡N), and carbon dioxide (CO₂).

image.alt: Shape of an sp hybrid orbital showing large and small lobes
image.caption: sp orbitals are highly directional with one large lobe for bonding

principles.application: The two unhybridized $p$ orbitals ($p_y, p_z$) are available for $\pi$ bonding, allowing for triple bond formation.
principles.analogy: Like having two hands (sp hybrids) to shake hands linearly, while holding two flags (p orbitals) sticking out sideways.

image.alt: Diagram showing orbital count conservation in sp hybridization
image.caption: Total orbitals before and after hybridization remain constant (4)

principles.statement: sp hybrids have 50% s-character, higher than sp² (33%) or sp³ (25%).
principles.application: Higher s-character means electrons are held closer to the nucleus, resulting in shorter, stronger, and more acidic C-H bonds.
principles.example: Ethyne C-H bond length (106 pm) < Ethene C-H (108 pm) < Ethane C-H (110 pm).

image.alt: Comparison of bond lengths based on s-character
image.caption: Higher s-character correlates with shorter bond lengths

components.definition: The sp hybrid wavefunction is an equal mix of s and p character: $\psi_{sp} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}(\phi_s + \phi_p)$
components.interpretation: The high s-character concentrates electron density near the nucleus, increasing electronegativity of the sp-hybridized carbon.

image.alt: Mathematical representation of sp hybrid wavefunction
image.caption: Equal contribution from s and p orbitals defines sp hybrids

classifications.value: Steric Number = 2 (2 sigma bonds, 0 lone pairs)
characteristics: Contains triple bonds or cumulative double bonds
classifications.behavior: Forms one sigma bond and two pi bonds with adjacent sp/sp²/sp³ atoms

examples.explanation: Each carbon forms 1 C-C sigma bond and 1 C-H sigma bond. Two p-orbitals form 2 pi bonds.

image.alt: Structure of ethyne showing linear geometry
image.caption: Ethyne (acetylene) is the simplest alkyne with sp carbons

examples.explanation: Central carbon is sp hybridized, forming two sigma bonds with oxygen. Two pi systems form perpendicular to each other.

worked_examples.title: Analyzing Bonding in Ethyne (C₂H₂)
worked_examples.problem: Determine the hybridization of carbon in ethyne, describe the orbital overlap involved in the C≡C triple bond, and predict the molecular geometry.

step_by_step.explanation: Each carbon is bonded to 1 H and 1 C. No lone pairs. Steric Number = 2.
Steric number 2 requires 2 hybrid orbitals. Mix one s and one p.
Carbon has three p orbitals total. One used in hybrid, two remain.
C-C sigma bond: $sp-sp$ overlap. C-H sigma bond: $sp-s$ overlap. C-C pi bonds: $p_y-p_y$ and $p_z-p_z$ sideways overlap.

worked_examples.final_answer: Carbon is sp hybridized. Geometry is linear (180°). The triple bond consists of one sigma bond and two perpendicular pi bonds.
worked_examples.concept_applied: sp hybridization enables linear geometry and multiple pi bonding capacity.

image.alt: Orbital overlap diagram for ethyne triple bond
image.caption: Sigma framework formed by sp hybrids; pi system formed by unhybridized p orbitals

process_flow.process: Identify Triple Bond or Linear Center
process_flow.description: Look for C≡C, C≡N, or central atoms with two double bonds (X=A=Y).
process_flow.mechanism: Triple bonds require two pi bonds, necessitating two unhybridized p orbitals.
process_flow.result: Candidate atoms identified for sp hybridization

image.alt: Examples of molecules with sp centers
image.caption: Identify atoms involved in triple bonds or linear arrangements

process_flow.description: Confirm the atom has exactly 2 electron domains (bonded atoms + lone pairs).

image.alt: Steric number calculation for sp atom
image.caption: Steric number of 2 confirms sp hybridization

process_flow.description: Orient the two unhybridized p orbitals perpendicular to the sigma bond axis.
process_flow.mechanism: Sideways overlap of parallel p orbitals forms pi bonds
process_flow.result: Cylindrical electron density around the sigma bond axis

image.alt: Perpendicular pi orbitals in sp hybridized system
image.caption: Two perpendicular pi bonds create cylindrical electron density

sections.role: Enabler of Triple Bonds and Linear Geometry

components.function: Form strong sigma bonds along the linear axis
components.key_feature: Highly directional, large lobe for effective overlap

image.alt: Detailed view of sp hybrid orbital lobes
image.caption: Asymmetric lobes maximize overlap efficiency

components.function: Form two pi bonds perpendicular to each other

image.alt: Two perpendicular p orbitals on sp carbon
image.caption: Unhybridized p orbitals are perpendicular to the sp axis

function.operation: sp hybrids form the rigid sigma framework; p orbitals create the reactive pi system.
function.energy: sp C-H bonds are stronger and more acidic than sp² or sp³ C-H bonds due to s-character.
function.mechanism: Linear arrangement minimizes steric hindrance; pi electrons are exposed for electrophilic attack.

image.alt: Function diagram showing sigma framework and pi reactivity
image.caption: Sigma bonds provide structure; pi bonds provide reactivity

cycle.operation: Head-on overlap of sp hybrids with partner atoms

image.alt: Sigma bond formation in sp system
image.caption: sp hybrids form the linear sigma backbone

cycle.operation: Sideways overlap of parallel unhybridized p orbitals
cycle.purpose: Creates triple bond character and electron richness

image.alt: Pi bond formation in sp system
image.caption: Two pi bonds surround the sigma bond axis

summary: sp hybridization involves the mixing of one $s$ and one $p$ orbital to create two linear $sp$ hybrid orbitals oriented $180^\circ$ apart. This leaves two unhybridized $p$ orbitals perpendicular to the hybrid axis, enabling the formation of two $\pi$ bonds alongside one $\sigma$ bond (a triple bond). sp-hybridized carbons exhibit 50% s-character, resulting in shorter, stronger bonds and increased acidity of attached hydrogens compared to sp² or sp³ carbons. This hybridization is fundamental to the structure and reactivity of alkynes and other linear molecular systems.
